subject: Re: Serena Boardman
body:
get more details


----- Original Message ----
From: Cecilia Steen <cecilia.steen@gmail.com>
To: JE <jeeproject@yahoo.com>
Sent: Tuesday, December 5, 2006 6:31:47 PM
Subject: Serena Boardman



Dear Lesley and /or Cecilia:
 
When one of you has a moment - could you please let Mr. Epstein know that the ex Lycee house at 12 East 73rd Street is going to be coming on the market shortly.
 
The house has good bones, is well located and has the bonus of being in close proximity to 9 East 71st Street.
 
In terms of condition - I have not yet seen it but understand that it has been in the process of being renovated so at least the mechanicals should be in order.  As for price - I believe that the ask is going to be $22.5 million which is not that far off given recent comparable sales in the low $20 millions and upper teens. 
 
Let me know if this house may be of interest to Mr. Epstein - if so I will make arrangements to show it to him before it comes on the market.
 
Thanks, in advance, for passing this information along.
 
Regards,
Serena Boardman
Sotheby's International Realty  
(212) 606-7611 work
(917) 667-2468 cell 
 
PS:  I now have a BlackBerry and am always easily reachable by email.

--- Cecilia Steen cecilia.steen@gmail.com wrote:

Dear Cecelia:

The asking price for 41 East 65th Street is
$17,250,000 and the building
is 28ft wide and quite imposing. That said the the
street is medium and
original details only remain in the front hall
(marble wall, marble
ceiling, and marble bench) and in the form of
several fireplace surrounds
throughout the building; the remainder is "office"
like and in reasonably
good and usable condition.

Also, please let Jeffrey know that there is a new
listing at 13 East 67th
Street. The house is not officially on the market
but I am going to see
it on Thursday. The property is approximately
20ft wide and has a nice
limestone facade. It is currently configured and
zoned as a single family
home and the condition is said to be be tired. I
will know more after I
see the house and will report. As for the price
the owner is said to want
about $11 million.

Once again, thanks for pasing all this information
along

Regards,
Serena
